# Benchmark Results

Performance measurements for the `a2a-protocol-sdk` Rust implementation,
generated by [Criterion.rs](https://github.com/bheisler/criterion.rs) statistical
benchmarking.

> **Note**: These numbers were collected on CI runners (`ubuntu-latest`). Absolute
> values will vary by hardware. Use these for **relative comparisons** between
> operations and for **regression detection** across releases — not as guarantees
> of production performance on your specific hardware.
>
> To reproduce on your own machine: `cargo bench -p a2a-benchmarks`

**Last updated:** 2026-03-21 01:16 UTC  
**Rust version:** rustc 1.94.0 (4a4ef493e 2026-03-02)  
**Platform:** Linux-x86_64  

## Transport Throughput

End-to-end HTTP round-trip latency through JSON-RPC and REST transports.
All measurements use loopback (127.0.0.1) to isolate SDK overhead from network latency.

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `transport_jsonrpc_send/single_message` | 1.66 ms |
| `transport_jsonrpc_stream/stream_drain` | 1.39 ms |
| `transport_payload_scaling/jsonrpc_send/1024` | 1.69 ms |
| `transport_payload_scaling/jsonrpc_send/16384` | 1.52 ms |
| `transport_payload_scaling/jsonrpc_send/256` | 1.69 ms |
| `transport_payload_scaling/jsonrpc_send/4096` | 1.52 ms |
| `transport_payload_scaling/jsonrpc_send/64` | 1.68 ms |
| `transport_rest_send/single_message` | 1.67 ms |
| `transport_rest_stream/stream_drain` | 1.37 ms |

## Protocol Overhead

Serialization and deserialization cost per A2A type. This is the baseline tax
every message pays regardless of transport.

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `protocol_batch/deserialize_tasks/1` | 1.4 µs |
| `protocol_batch/deserialize_tasks/10` | 16.6 µs |
| `protocol_batch/deserialize_tasks/100` | 163.7 µs |
| `protocol_batch/deserialize_tasks/50` | 83.0 µs |
| `protocol_batch/serialize_tasks/1` | 466 ns |
| `protocol_batch/serialize_tasks/10` | 3.9 µs |
| `protocol_batch/serialize_tasks/100` | 35.7 µs |
| `protocol_batch/serialize_tasks/50` | 18.5 µs |
| `protocol_jsonrpc_envelope/deserialize_request` | 929 ns |
| `protocol_jsonrpc_envelope/deserialize_response` | 2.0 µs |
| `protocol_jsonrpc_envelope/serialize_request` | 289 ns |
| `protocol_jsonrpc_envelope/serialize_response` | 580 ns |
| `protocol_stream_events/artifact_update_deserialize` | 693 ns |
| `protocol_stream_events/artifact_update_serialize` | 273 ns |
| `protocol_stream_events/status_update_deserialize` | 382 ns |
| `protocol_stream_events/status_update_serialize` | 110 ns |
| `protocol_type_serde/agent_card_deserialize` | 1.4 µs |
| `protocol_type_serde/agent_card_serialize` | 597 ns |
| `protocol_type_serde/message_deserialize/254` | 1.2 µs |
| `protocol_type_serde/message_serialize/254` | 352 ns |
| `protocol_type_serde/task_deserialize/295` | 1.4 µs |
| `protocol_type_serde/task_serialize/295` | 464 ns |

## Task Lifecycle

TaskStore and EventQueue operations — the backbone of task management.

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `lifecycle_e2e/send_and_complete` | 1.63 ms |
| `lifecycle_e2e/stream_and_drain` | 2.69 ms |
| `lifecycle_queue/write_read/1` | 770 ns |
| `lifecycle_queue/write_read/10` | 4.4 µs |
| `lifecycle_queue/write_read/100` | 42.4 µs |
| `lifecycle_queue/write_read/50` | 20.9 µs |
| `lifecycle_store_get/lookup_in_1000` | 436 ns |
| `lifecycle_store_list/filtered_page_50_of_250` | 23.1 µs |
| `lifecycle_store_save/single_task` | 660 ns |

## Concurrent Agents

Scaling behavior under parallel load — how latency changes as
concurrency increases from 1 to 64 simultaneous operations.

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `concurrent_mixed/send_then_get` | 1.59 ms |
| `concurrent_sends/jsonrpc/1` | 1.69 ms |
| `concurrent_sends/jsonrpc/16` | 5.63 ms |
| `concurrent_sends/jsonrpc/4` | 2.99 ms |
| `concurrent_sends/jsonrpc/64` | 13.94 ms |
| `concurrent_store/save_and_get/1` | 29.8 µs |
| `concurrent_store/save_and_get/16` | 74.2 µs |
| `concurrent_store/save_and_get/4` | 28.6 µs |
| `concurrent_store/save_and_get/64` | 200.6 µs |
| `concurrent_streams/jsonrpc/1` | 1.41 ms |
| `concurrent_streams/jsonrpc/16` | 6.52 ms |
| `concurrent_streams/jsonrpc/4` | 3.93 ms |
| `concurrent_streams/jsonrpc/64` | 12.49 ms |

## Error Paths

Cost of error handling — comparing happy path latency to error path latency.
Production systems spend significant time on error paths; benchmarking only
the happy path gives an incomplete picture.

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `errors_happy_vs_error/error_path` | 1.52 ms |
| `errors_happy_vs_error/happy_path` | 1.64 ms |
| `errors_malformed_request/invalid_json` | 95.7 µs |
| `errors_malformed_request/wrong_content_type` | 92.7 µs |
| `errors_task_not_found/get_nonexistent_task` | 107.9 µs |

## Streaming & Backpressure

Stream throughput under varying event volumes and consumer speeds.
Reveals buffering and flow-control overhead that synthetic single-event tests miss.

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `backpressure_concurrent_streams/streams/1` | 1.49 ms |
| `backpressure_concurrent_streams/streams/16` | 5.93 ms |
| `backpressure_concurrent_streams/streams/4` | 4.16 ms |
| `backpressure_slow_consumer/1ms_delay` | 45.55 ms |
| `backpressure_slow_consumer/5ms_delay` | 130.49 ms |
| `backpressure_slow_consumer/fast_consumer` | 1.48 ms |
| `backpressure_stream_volume/101_events` | 1.62 ms |
| `backpressure_stream_volume/11_events` | 1.45 ms |
| `backpressure_stream_volume/3_events` | 1.40 ms |
| `backpressure_stream_volume/51_events` | 1.59 ms |

## Data Volume Scaling

Shows how store operations scale as data accumulates over time.

| `data_volume_get/lookup/100000` | 203 ns |
| `data_volume_history_depth/save_with_turns/1` | 1.3 µs |
| `data_volume_history_depth/save_with_turns/10` | 4.4 µs |
| `data_volume_history_depth/save_with_turns/20` | 9.4 µs |
| `data_volume_history_depth/save_with_turns/5` | 2.3 µs |
| `data_volume_history_depth/save_with_turns/50` | 19.7 µs |
| `data_volume_list/filtered_page_50/1000` | 23.6 µs |
| `data_volume_list/filtered_page_50/10000` | 23.8 µs |
| `data_volume_list/filtered_page_50/100000` | 24.6 µs |

## Memory Overhead

Heap allocation counts and bytes per operation, measured via a counting
allocator (`#[global_allocator]`). Values represent allocation counts or
bytes — not time — encoded as nanoseconds for Criterion tracking.

| Metric | Unit |
|--------|------|
| `*_alloc_count` | Number of `alloc()` calls per operation |
| `*_bytes_per_payload` | Bytes allocated per operation |

| Benchmark | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| `memory_bytes_per_payload/serialize_bytes/1024` | 910 |
| `memory_bytes_per_payload/serialize_bytes/16384` | 11031 |
| `memory_bytes_per_payload/serialize_bytes/256` | 351 |
| `memory_bytes_per_payload/serialize_bytes/4096` | 2949 |
| `memory_bytes_per_payload/serialize_bytes/64` | 212 |
| `memory_deserialize/agent_card_alloc_count` | 1381 |
| `memory_deserialize/task_alloc_count` | 1355 |
| `memory_history_scaling/deserialize_allocs/1` | 1713 |
| `memory_history_scaling/deserialize_allocs/10` | 6984 |
| `memory_history_scaling/deserialize_allocs/20` | 13304 |
| `memory_history_scaling/deserialize_allocs/5` | 3973 |
| `memory_history_scaling/deserialize_allocs/50` | 31775 |
| `memory_history_scaling/serialize_allocs/1` | 554 |
| `memory_history_scaling/serialize_allocs/10` | 2500 |
| `memory_history_scaling/serialize_allocs/20` | 6096 |
| `memory_history_scaling/serialize_allocs/5` | 1491 |
| `memory_history_scaling/serialize_allocs/50` | 14600 |
| `memory_serialize/agent_card_alloc_count` | 546 |
| `memory_serialize/task_alloc_count` | 400 |

## Cross-Language Comparison

Standardized workloads designed to be reproduced identically across all
A2A SDK implementations (Python, Go, JS, Java, C#/.NET).

- All SDKs hit the **same Rust echo server** (eliminates server-side variance)
- All workloads use **identical JSON payloads** from `benches/cross_language/`
- Results use **median ± MAD** to resist outlier pollution

| Benchmark | Median |
|-----------|--------|
| `cross_language_concurrent_50/rust` | 10.34 ms |
| `cross_language_echo_roundtrip/rust` | 1.69 ms |
| `cross_language_minimal_overhead/rust` | 1.58 ms |
| `cross_language_serialize_agent_card/rust_deserialize` | 1.4 µs |
| `cross_language_serialize_agent_card/rust_roundtrip` | 2.1 µs |
| `cross_language_serialize_agent_card/rust_serialize` | 600 ns |
| `cross_language_stream_events/rust` | 1.38 ms |

---

## Methodology

All benchmarks use [Criterion.rs](https://github.com/bheisler/criterion.rs),
which provides:

- **Statistical significance testing** — detects real regressions vs noise
- **Warm-up iterations** — avoids cold-start measurement artifacts
- **Median ± MAD** — robust central tendency resistant to outliers
- **Configurable sample sizes** — more iterations for noisy benchmarks

### What we benchmark

The SDK's value proposition is the **A2A protocol layer and runtime efficiency**,
not agent logic. We benchmark what the SDK owns: transport overhead, serialization
cost, store operations, concurrency scaling, streaming backpressure, error handling,
and memory allocation behavior.

### What we do NOT benchmark

- **Agent intelligence** — LLM quality is an eval problem, not a perf benchmark
- **Network latency** — all benchmarks use loopback (127.0.0.1)
- **TLS handshake** — benchmarks use plaintext HTTP
- **Task completion quality** — needs human-preference evaluation

### Reproducing locally

```bash
# Run all benchmarks
cargo bench -p a2a-benchmarks

# Run a specific module
cargo bench -p a2a-benchmarks --bench transport_throughput

# Save baseline, make changes, then compare
./benches/scripts/run_benchmarks.sh --save
# ... make changes ...
./benches/scripts/run_benchmarks.sh --compare
```

Full HTML reports (with violin plots and comparison overlays) are generated
in `target/criterion/`.
